automotive-ev-battery.title: EU lawmakers target tax breaks for ICE company cars
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The European Union plans to phase out tax incentives for combustion-engine company cars while redirecting support exclusively to European-made electric vehicles. This policy shift represents a significant regulatory move to accelerate EV adoption across the bloc, though it has drawn alarm from the automotive industry.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Basquevolt launches lithium-metal battery cell
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Spanish battery manufacturer Basquevolt has unveiled its new BQV400L battery cell featuring a lithium-metal anode and rated among Europe's highest-performance battery cells currently in production. The cell is immediately available and represents a significant advancement in European battery technology for EV applications.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Gotion Unveils Sodium Battery Products With 261 Wh/kg Energy Density And 20,000 Charge Cycles
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese battery manufacturer Gotion has introduced three new sodium battery products with an energy density of 261 Wh/kg and capacity for 20,000 charge cycles. This development provides an alternative battery chemistry option for EV manufacturers seeking cost-effective energy storage solutions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Uber and JSW partner on EVs for India
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Uber and India's JSW Group have formed a strategic partnership to develop and deploy electric vehicles tailored for India's ride-hailing market. The collaboration aims to create EV solutions specifically designed for Indian riders, drivers, and fleet operators.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New York awards $6 million for vehicle-grid integration projects
automotive-ev-battery.summary: New York State has distributed over $6 million in funding across seven vehicle-grid integration projects aimed at enhancing EV charging flexibility and reducing grid costs. The initiatives support bidirectional charging and diverse applications including refuse trucks, wireless charging, and thermal energy storage.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BMW launches pre-series production for central control unit of iX5 Hydrogen
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW has commenced pre-series production of the "Energy Master" central control unit for its hydrogen fuel cell drive system at its Landshut facility. Simultaneously, the plant is expanding production capacity for battery-electric control units used in BMW's Neue Klasse models.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Bolt launches EV ride-hailing category in South Africa
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Ride-hailing company Bolt has established a dedicated electric vehicle category in South Africa through a partnership with YugoRide, beginning operations in Cape Town. The company plans to deploy 500 EVs across the country by the end of 2026.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 92 New EV Chargers At 14 Charging Stations Announced
automotive-ev-battery.summary: EV charger companies Kempower and Blink Charging are collaborating to install 92 new charging ports across 14 stations, with two locations already operational and twelve additional sites planned for East Coast deployment in 2026. This expansion supports the growing infrastructure needs of the EV market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD upgrades Atto 3 with Flash Charging capability in China
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has introduced the third generation of its Atto 3 compact electric vehicle in China under the name Yuan Plus, equipped with Flash Charging capability and other improvements. Pricing in the Chinese market begins at approximately 15,200 euros.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD delivers 23 new electric buses to Mallorca
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Spanish public transport operator EMT Palma has received 23 new electric buses from BYD for deployment across multiple routes in Mallorca. The delivery represents part of an initial batch within a larger procurement program totaling 113 vehicles.

world-news.title: Russia's Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
world-news.summary: Russia has accused Ukraine of striking a student dormitory, with President Putin vowing retaliation, while Ukraine claims it targeted Russia's elite Rubicon drone military unit in Moscow-occupied eastern Ukraine. The escalating accusations underscore intensifying military operations in the ongoing conflict.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Tehran says diplomacy continues but no deal yet with US
world-news.summary: Iran reports that diplomatic negotiations with the United States are ongoing but no agreement has been reached, while Israeli attacks on Lebanon have resulted in over 3,100 deaths and 9,400 injuries since March. The escalating regional tensions involve multiple state actors and pose significant geopolitical risks.

world-news.title: Rubio tries to reassure NATO allies over US troop deployments
world-news.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has sought to reassure NATO allies regarding US military deployments, following President Trump's announcement of plans to increase troop presence in Poland after recent deployment cancellations. The statements reflect shifting US military commitments in Eastern Europe amid geopolitical tensions.

world-news.title: Ebola risk raised to 'very high' in DR Congo
world-news.summary: The UN health agency has raised the Ebola risk assessment to 'very high' in the Democratic Republic of Congo, though the global risk remains low. The escalation reflects growing concern over disease spread in the affected region.

world-news.title: Ebola outbreak disrupts life along the DRC-Uganda border
world-news.summary: With nearly 750 suspected Ebola cases reported in the Democratic Republic of Congo, health authorities have intensified preventive measures along the DRC-Uganda border. The outbreak poses significant public health and cross-border security concerns for the region.

world-news.title: Bolivia in crisis: Social unrest, demands for president to resign escalate
world-news.summary: Bolivia faces escalating social unrest with protesters demanding the resignation of President Rodrigo Paz, signaling a significant domestic political crisis. The instability may have broader implications for regional stability in South America.

world-news.title: Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse by Israeli forces while detained
world-news.summary: International activists detained after attempting to reach Gaza have alleged they were subjected to physical and sexual abuse by Israeli forces, though Israel's prison service denies the accusations. The incident reflects ongoing tensions surrounding access to the Gaza Strip.

world-news.title: Carney says Alberta is 'essential' to Canada as province plans vote on separation
world-news.summary: Alberta has announced plans to hold a referendum on provincial separation, prompting federal officials to emphasize the province's importance to Canada's national unity. The political development represents a significant constitutional and economic challenge for the country.

world-news.title: Mahmoud Khalil to appeal US deportation case to Supreme Court
world-news.summary: Pro-Palestine advocate Mahmoud Khalil is pursuing an appeal to the US Supreme Court, claiming that the Trump administration targeted him for protected political speech and subsequent deportation. The case raises significant questions about civil liberties and government overreach.

world-news.title: Tulsi Gabbard to resign as US director of national intelligence
world-news.summary: Tulsi Gabbard, serving as US director of national intelligence, has announced her resignation, citing her husband's illness as the reason. The departure represents a significant change in the Trump administration's intelligence leadership.

finance-markets.title: Wall Street bets on 2026 rate rise as Kevin Warsh takes charge of the Fed
finance-markets.summary: Markets are pricing in expectations for the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates by the end of 2026 following Kevin Warsh's appointment as Fed chair, with geopolitical tensions in Iran adding inflationary pressures. This represents a significant shift in monetary policy expectations and has broad implications for financial markets, borrowing costs, and economic growth.

finance-markets.title: Nasdaq Gets Approval From SEC to List Bitcoin Index Options
finance-markets.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission has approved Nasdaq to list index options based on Bitcoin's price, marking another step toward mainstream financial integration of digital assets. This regulatory approval signals growing acceptance of cryptocurrency derivatives among institutional investors.

finance-markets.title: Lantheus Explores Potential $7 Billion Sale Following Offer From Curium
finance-markets.summary: Lantheus Holdings is evaluating a takeover offer from Curium valuing the radiopharmaceutical company at approximately $7 billion. The potential transaction reflects continued consolidation activity in the healthcare and specialty pharma sector.

finance-markets.title: Trump administration to make foreigners leave US to apply for green cards
finance-markets.summary: The Trump administration is implementing stricter permanent residency requirements that will force foreign nationals to leave the United States to apply for green cards. The policy change could significantly disrupt business operations and labor markets, particularly in sectors reliant on immigrant workers.

finance-markets.title: Emerging Assets Post Positive Week as Hopes for Peace Deal Grow
finance-markets.summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies gained during the week as investor sentiment improved on expectations that the United States and Iran are progressing toward a peace agreement. The potential resolution of geopolitical tensions has provided support for risk assets in developing economies.

finance-markets.title: Grocery Stores Feel Crunch of Fuel, Labor Costs Ahead of Memorial Day
finance-markets.summary: Grocery retailers are experiencing margin pressure from elevated fuel and labor costs as they head into the Memorial Day holiday period, with prices expected to remain elevated despite hopes for eventual fuel price moderation. Consumer demand for holiday purchases persists, but retailers warn that structural labor cost inflation will continue to pressure pricing.

finance-markets.title: If you think you understand bonds, you don't
finance-markets.summary: This educational feature outlines five common pitfalls investors encounter when evaluating bond investments, recognizing the complexity of fixed-income markets. The analysis provides guidance for avoiding typical mistakes in bond portfolio management and valuation.

finance-markets.title: Pakistan army chief travels to Tehran in push for US-Iran deal
finance-markets.summary: Pakistan's army chief has traveled to Iran as part of international mediation efforts, alongside Qatar and other parties, to prevent an escalation of US-Iran tensions toward full-scale conflict. The diplomatic initiative underscores global concern about the geopolitical and economic fallout from intensified Middle East hostilities.

finance-markets.title: Trump's risky military options to break Iran deadlock
finance-markets.summary: The Trump administration is considering various military operations, including air strikes and naval actions, as potential means to pressure Iran into policy concessions, though analysts note no straightforward path to achieving stated objectives. The uncertainty surrounding military escalation continues to add risk premium to energy markets and geopolitical-sensitive assets.

finance-markets.title: Equatorial Leans Against Bidding on Copasa Privatization
finance-markets.summary: Equatorial SA has signaled it is unlikely to submit a bid for a controlling stake in Copasa, a Brazilian water utility undergoing privatization by the state of Minas Gerais. The decision reduces competition in the privatization process and may affect the final valuation and terms of the asset sale.

science-space.title: SpaceX just launched Starship V3 — its most powerful megarocket yet — into space for the 1st time in spectacular Flight 12 test
science-space.summary: SpaceX successfully launched Starship V3, the most powerful rocket ever built, in its maiden flight test. The achievement represents a major milestone in commercial spaceflight and reusable rocket development.

science-space.title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
science-space.summary: NASA researchers at Glenn Research Center have discovered and tested a new material that could enable astronauts to melt moon rocks and extract metals and oxygen from lunar resources. This advancement supports NASA's long-term goal of sustainable human exploration and habitation on the Moon.

science-space.title: NASA is updating its Artemis moon base plan
science-space.summary: NASA will provide an update on its lunar base plans as part of the Artemis program on May 26. The briefing will outline NASA's strategy for establishing sustained human presence on the Moon.

science-space.title: Webb Studies Star Clusters
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has completed a comprehensive study of nearly 9,000 star clusters across four nearby galaxies, including observations of Messier 51. The research reveals new insights into how massive star clusters form and evolve.

science-space.title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
science-space.summary: NASA announced a comprehensive agency reorganization designed to improve efficiency and accelerate mission delivery in alignment with national space policy priorities. The restructuring positions the agency to better meet high-priority objectives with greater speed and accountability.

science-space.title: NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management
science-space.summary: NASA announced it will open competition for the next contract managing the Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California, breaking nearly a century of exclusive management by the California Institute of Technology. The move aims to ensure continued accountability and fiscal stewardship of the federally funded research center.

science-space.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
science-space.summary: NASA will provide live coverage of a Roscosmos spacewalk scheduled for May 27 outside the International Space Station, with two cosmonauts conducting approximately five hours of extravehicular activity. The event will be streamed across multiple NASA platforms including NASA+, Amazon Prime, and YouTube.

science-space.title: An ancient solar storm left clues in tree rings and a famous poet's diary: 'Red lights in the northern sky'
science-space.summary: Scientists have identified evidence of a powerful ancient solar storm through analysis of tree rings and historical records, including a Japanese poet's diary account of mysterious red lights in the sky over Kyoto. The discovery provides new understanding of historical space weather events and their terrestrial effects.

science-space.title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
science-space.summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured an image of a distant galaxy cluster containing galaxies of various shapes and sizes, demonstrating Hubble's continued capability to resolve fine astronomical detail. The image showcases characteristic diffraction spikes from foreground stars in the field of view.

science-space.title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
science-space.summary: NASA has issued a formal call for proposals from filmmakers, documentarians, and other creative professionals to develop storytelling content about agency missions including the Artemis lunar program and advanced propulsion technologies. The announcement reflects NASA's commitment to public engagement and mission communication.

tech-ai.title: AI is being used to resurrect the voices of dead pilots
tech-ai.summary: Researchers have used artificial intelligence to reconstruct pilot voices from spectrograms of cockpit recordings, prompting the National Transportation Safety Board to temporarily restrict access to its accident investigation docket. The incident raises serious concerns about the misuse of AI technology and the security of sensitive aviation safety data.

tech-ai.title: We tried Google's AI glasses and they're almost there
tech-ai.summary: Google demonstrated prototype Android XR glasses that integrate Gemini-powered artificial intelligence for real-time translation, navigation, and contextual information overlaid directly in the user's field of view. The technology represents a significant step toward practical augmented reality computing devices.

tech-ai.title: You can no longer Google the word 'disregard'
tech-ai.summary: Google's AI Overviews feature has encountered a critical bug where searching for the term "disregard" breaks the search interface, returning inappropriate chatbot-like responses instead of relevant results. The malfunction highlights ongoing reliability issues with the company's AI-powered search functionality.

tech-ai.title: How VCs and founders use inflated 'ARR' to crown AI startups
tech-ai.summary: Several AI startups are employing questionable accounting practices to inflate annual recurring revenue metrics when communicating with the public, with venture capital investors reportedly aware of these inflated figures. The trend raises transparency and accountability concerns within the artificial intelligence investment community.

tech-ai.title: Waymo suspends freeway driving amid safety concerns
tech-ai.summary: Waymo has suspended freeway autonomous driving capabilities across all U.S. markets due to safety concerns related to construction zones, and also paused services in Atlanta and San Antonio following issues with flooded roads. The suspension marks a setback for the autonomous vehicle company's expansion efforts.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX launches Starship V3 for the first time, but loses booster on return
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX successfully completed its first orbital flight of the upgraded Starship V3 rocket, though the mission ended with the loss of the booster during its return phase. The vehicle is critical to SpaceX's ambitious goals for lunar missions and Mars exploration in the coming years.

tech-ai.title: Google appeals search monopoly ruling, says it won business 'fair and square'
tech-ai.summary: Google filed its formal appeal of the federal court ruling that declared it an illegal search monopolist, arguing that the decision violated legal standards and that it achieved its market position through legitimate competition. The appeal challenges both the August 2024 monopolization decision and the September 2025 remedies ruling.

tech-ai.title: Meta's Forum is part Reddit, part Facebook, and part Google AI Overview
tech-ai.summary: Meta has launched Forum, a dedicated iPhone application that combines Facebook Groups functionality with an integrated AI chatbot to assist users in searching, viewing, and posting advice within their groups. The app represents Meta's effort to compete with Reddit and traditional search by creating a unified community discussion platform.

tech-ai.title: Elon, stop trying to make Grok happen
tech-ai.summary: According to a Reuters analysis of federal government AI usage records, Elon Musk's xAI chatbot Grok has minimal adoption and appears rarely in government procurement, despite Musk positioning it as central to a potential major initial public offering. The limited uptake suggests significant challenges for the platform's commercial viability.

tech-ai.title: Apple says Epic lawsuit shouldn't reshape App Store rules for all developers
tech-ai.summary: Apple has filed arguments with the Supreme Court requesting that the injunction won by Epic Games be narrowed and that a contempt ruling regarding external payment options be overturned. The company contends that the lower court's ruling should not apply broadly to all developers on its platform.

health-biotech.title: FDA grants Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca's Datroway a key breast cancer approval
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has approved Datroway, a TROP2-directed antibody-drug conjugate developed by Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca, as a first-line treatment option for triple-negative breast cancer. This approval represents the drug's third indication and positions the companies competitively against rival Gilead in the breast cancer therapeutics market.

health-biotech.title: AstraZeneca wins EU backing for breast cancer drug, splitting with FDA panel
health-biotech.summary: AstraZeneca has secured a positive opinion from European regulators for its breast cancer drug, marking a divergence from an FDA advisory committee that voted against the same drug last month. The split approval highlights differing regulatory standards between the EU and US markets.

health-biotech.title: Biogen, Denali to drop drug in non-genetic Parkinson's after mid-stage study flop
health-biotech.summary: Biogen and Denali Therapeutics have discontinued their LRRK2 inhibitor BIIB122 in early Parkinson's disease following a failed Phase 2b trial. The setback represents a significant disappointment in the competitive field of Parkinson's disease therapeutics.

health-biotech.title: FDA clears Gilead's hepatitis D drug, four years after prior rejection
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has granted approval to Gilead's hepatitis D treatment Hepcludex (bulevirtide), ending a four-year regulatory journey that included a previous rejection due to manufacturing and distribution concerns. The clearance expands treatment options for the rare and serious hepatitis D infection.

health-biotech.title: Congress slashed Medicaid funding to providers. The Trump administration wants to cut even further
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration has announced plans to reduce state-directed payments to healthcare providers beyond previous congressional cuts to Medicaid funding. The proposal is expected to trigger opposition from provider advocacy groups.

health-biotech.title: Novartis says Avidity's science could apply beyond muscular dystrophies
health-biotech.summary: Novartis is exploring applications of Avidity Biosciences' gene therapy technology beyond muscular dystrophy following its $12 billion acquisition. The effort aims to maximize the return on investment by identifying additional disease indications for the underlying science.

health-biotech.title: Longevity startup Retro Biosciences says latest fundraising values it at $1.8 billion
health-biotech.summary: Retro Biosciences, the longevity-focused startup backed by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, has completed a new funding round valuing the company at $1.8 billion. The capital raise underscores investor interest in companies pursuing lifespan extension technologies.

health-biotech.title: Genentech is soliciting research that can be used to blunt pharma reforms in Washington
health-biotech.summary: Genentech is actively soliciting academic research from scientists with the apparent goal of generating evidence to counter proposed pharmaceutical industry reforms in Congress. The strategy reflects the industry's efforts to shape the regulatory and legislative landscape.

health-biotech.title: How Utah's AI prescribing experiment is going so far
health-biotech.summary: Utah has released preliminary data from a pilot program using artificial intelligence to automate prescription renewals for chronic disease management. Since mid-December, the system has been processing renewals through Doctronic's platform.

health-biotech.title: GOP lawmaker asks Trump administration to curb China biotech deals
health-biotech.summary: A prominent Republican congressman has urged the Trump administration to restrict biotech investment flows into China, citing national security concerns. The request threatens to disrupt significant dealmaking activity involving US investment firms and pharmaceutical companies.

cybersecurity.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
cybersecurity.summary: Congressional lawmakers are demanding an investigation into a significant data breach at the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency after a contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud credentials and classified agency secrets on a public GitHub repository. CISA continues efforts to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials, raising concerns about the security of critical infrastructure oversight.

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers have exploited known vulnerabilities in legacy Internet routers to conduct large-scale harvesting of Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The sophisticated campaign enabled state-backed actors to steal credentials without deploying malware, representing a significant espionage operation.

cybersecurity.title: Cisco Patches CVSS 10.0 Secure Workload REST API Flaw Enabling Data Access
cybersecurity.summary: Cisco has released critical security updates for a maximum-severity vulnerability in its Secure Workload product that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to access sensitive data through insufficient API authentication controls. The CVE-2026-20223 flaw, rated at CVSS 10.0, poses an immediate risk to organizations using the affected platform.

cybersecurity.title: Megalodon GitHub Attack Targets 5,561 Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows
cybersecurity.summary: A large-scale automated attack campaign named Megalodon has injected malicious GitHub Actions workflows into over 5,500 repositories, pushing 5,718 compromised commits across multiple accounts within a six-hour window. The attackers used forged identities and base64-encoded payloads designed to exfiltrate sensitive data from CI/CD pipelines.

cybersecurity.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
cybersecurity.summary: A 24-year-old British member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and identity theft following his involvement in 2022 phishing attacks that compromised major technology companies and stole tens of millions in cryptocurrency. The guilty plea marks a significant prosecution success against the notorious hacking collective.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released 167 critical security patches addressing vulnerabilities in Windows operating systems and related products, including a zero-day in SharePoint Server and the actively exploited BlueHammer flaw in Windows Defender. Major vendors including Google and Adobe also released emergency updates addressing zero-day vulnerabilities and remote code execution flaws.

cybersecurity.title: First VPN Dismantled in Global Takedown Over Use by 25 Ransomware Groups
cybersecurity.summary: International law enforcement authorities led by France and the Netherlands have successfully dismantled the First VPN service, which was actively used by approximately 25 ransomware groups to conceal the origins of attacks including ransomware distribution, data theft, and denial-of-service campaigns. The takedown represents a coordinated effort to disrupt critical criminal infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Adds Exploited Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One Vulnerabilities to KEV
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has added two actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including a critical origin validation flaw in Langflow with a CVSS score of 9.4. The additions underscore the immediate threat posed by these vulnerabilities and the need for rapid patching by affected organizations.

cybersecurity.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukraine Government Entities with Prometheus Phishing Malware
cybersecurity.summary: The Belarus-aligned threat actor Ghostwriter has launched phishing campaigns targeting Ukrainian government organizations using lures related to the Prometheus online learning platform. The attacks, documented by Ukraine's Computer Emergency Response Team, demonstrate continued efforts by state-sponsored actors to compromise critical government infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
cybersecurity.summary: A Chinese advanced persistent threat group has employed Discord and Microsoft Graph APIs alongside SOCKS proxies to conduct espionage operations against European government entities. The sophisticated attack infrastructure highlights evolving tactics used by state-sponsored actors to maintain persistence and evade detection within sensitive networks.

crypto-web3.title: Congress hits Polymarket and Kalshi with a massive insider trading probe
crypto-web3.summary: House Majority Representative James Comer has launched a formal investigation into prediction market platforms Polymarket and Kalshi, demanding internal records from both companies over concerns that government employees may be using classified information for trading profits. The probe represents significant regulatory scrutiny of the emerging prediction market sector and raises questions about information security and market integrity.

crypto-web3.title: House Oversight chair launches probe into insider trading on Kalshi and Polymarket
crypto-web3.summary: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer has initiated a formal investigation into potential insider trading activities on prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket, requesting internal documentation with a June 5 deadline. The probe focuses on whether government officials are exploiting access to classified information to generate illicit trading profits.

crypto-web3.title: SEC delays tokenized asset exemption amid concerns over third-party tokens
crypto-web3.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission has postponed implementation of its anticipated exemption for tokenized assets, citing unresolved concerns regarding third-party tokens and regulatory clarity. The delay signals ongoing regulatory caution regarding token classifications and their treatment under securities law.

crypto-web3.title: New strategic bitcoin reserve bill drops 1 million BTC purchase target, adds 20-year lockup period
crypto-web3.summary: A revised strategic bitcoin reserve proposal has reduced its initial purchase target while introducing a 20-year asset lockup requirement and mandating quarterly public proof-of-reserve disclosures with third-party audits. The bill represents a significant policy development regarding potential government-backed cryptocurrency holdings.

crypto-web3.title: Kalshi, Polymarket lose bids to halt Nevada and Washington gambling cases
crypto-web3.summary: The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that federal derivatives market oversight does not provide legal exemption for prediction market platforms from state-level gaming enforcement actions in Nevada and Washington. The decision expands regulatory exposure for decentralized prediction markets to state gambling authorities.

crypto-web3.title: Robinhood Crypto COO Tanya Denisova is leaving company amid revenue slowdown
crypto-web3.summary: Tanya Denisova, Chief Operating Officer of Robinhood's cryptocurrency division, is departing the company after more than five years as the trading platform faces declining digital asset revenues and seeks to reduce exposure to cryptocurrency market cyclicality. The executive departure reflects ongoing challenges within the retail crypto trading sector.

crypto-web3.title: SEC Commissioner Peirce counters views that crypto rule will foster synthetic tokens
crypto-web3.summary: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce, who leads the agency's Crypto Task Force, has clarified positions regarding a delayed regulatory proposal to address misconceptions about its potential to encourage synthetic token creation. Peirce's statements aim to provide regulatory clarity on token treatment and asset classification standards.

crypto-web3.title: Tom Emmer brushes off law enforcement concerns over Clarity Act
crypto-web3.summary: House Majority Whip Tom Emmer dismissed concerns raised by law enforcement agencies regarding developer protections in the proposed Clarity Act, characterizing their objections as overstated. The disagreement highlights ongoing tensions between industry advocates and law enforcement regarding cryptocurrency regulation.

crypto-web3.title: Binance disputes latest WSJ report on alleged Iran-linked transactions
crypto-web3.summary: Binance has contested a Wall Street Journal report alleging that Iranian financier Babak Zanjani moved approximately $850 million through the exchange, with CEO Richard Teng asserting the narrative was mischaracterized. The dispute underscores ongoing scrutiny of cryptocurrency platforms regarding sanctions compliance and illicit fund flows.

crypto-web3.title: Why Minnesota is empowering local banks to fight Wall Street for crypto revenue
crypto-web3.summary: Minnesota's financial institutions are leveraging state-level initiatives to compete with major Wall Street players in the cryptocurrency sector, as local bankers determine they cannot remain sidelined from digital asset expansion. The development reflects growing momentum among regional financial institutions to participate in the crypto economy.

startups-vc.title: 20VC x SaaStr: Anthropic at $900B+, Salesforce Spends $300M on Tokens, SpaceX Files for the Biggest IPO in History, and Why the Tech Lash Is Just Beginning
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic closed a $30 billion funding round at a $900 billion valuation, nearly tripling its February valuation, while SpaceX filed for what would be the largest IPO in American history. The developments underscore the continued concentration of capital in AI and space technology, with major tech companies like Salesforce investing heavily in AI infrastructure through token purchases.

startups-vc.title: Elon Musk can't hear you over the sound of his $1.75 trillion IPO
startups-vc.summary: SpaceX filed its S-1 registration statement with the SEC, seeking a $1.75 trillion valuation that would make it the largest IPO in American history, featuring a $28 trillion total addressable market and compensation tied to establishing a Mars colony. The 36-page risk factors document reveals the ambitious scope of the company's operations and growth expectations.

startups-vc.title: Venture Capital Is Concentrating Faster Than Ever. What Happens To Everyone Else?
startups-vc.summary: U.S. venture capital concentration has accelerated dramatically, with 80% of startup investment through April 2026 going to rounds of $500 million and above, matching all of 2025's funding totals in just four months. The trend highlights growing disparities in capital access across the startup ecosystem as mega-rounds continue to dominate funding activity.

startups-vc.title: How VCs and founders use inflated 'ARR' to crown AI startups
startups-vc.summary: Some AI startups are employing non-traditional accounting methods to inflate their annual recurring revenue metrics, with venture capitalists reportedly aware of and complicit in these practices. The trend raises questions about the true financial health and sustainability of heavily hyped AI companies seeking validation through inflated performance claims.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Massive Deals For Medical Devices, Futuristic AI Gadgets And Frontier Labs Lead
startups-vc.summary: This week's largest funding rounds showcased significant activity across medical devices, AI hardware, aerospace and defense, fintech, and retail technology sectors. The deals reflect continued investor appetite for frontier technologies and specialized hardware solutions beyond traditional software platforms.

startups-vc.title: Where Funded Founders Went To School: 2026 Edition
startups-vc.summary: Crunchbase's annual survey found that alumni of the most selective U.S. universities continue to secure a disproportionately high share of startup funding rounds, reinforcing educational pedigree as a significant factor in venture capital allocation. The findings underscore ongoing disparities in founder funding access based on educational background.

startups-vc.title: The Modern GTM Org in 2026: 20-30% Leaner, 9x Flatter, ~2x More Net New Revenue Per Rep.
startups-vc.summary: ICONIQ Growth's latest benchmark report surveying 150+ B2B software companies reveals significant operational shifts, with modern go-to-market organizations operating 20-30% leaner, with flatter structures, and generating roughly double the net new revenue per sales representative. The findings align with widespread adoption of AI productivity tools, which have created a $270K per representative productivity gap.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare, a Y Combinator alum, completed its initial public offering, marking a significant milestone for the Missouri-based construction equipment operating system. The company has expanded from its regional origins to 373 locations nationwide, transforming construction operations through contractor-focused technology.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Learnings from HubSpot at $3.5B in ARR: 23% Reported Growth, $211M in Buybacks, and a 16% Stock Drop
startups-vc.summary: HubSpot reported Q1 2026 revenue of $881 million with 23% reported growth and achieved a $3.45 billion ARR run-rate, beating consensus estimates while conducting $211 million in stock buybacks. Despite strong operational metrics, the company's stock declined 16%, suggesting investor concerns about growth trajectory relative to market expectations.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: A Law Firm Operating System, Building Defense Tech Near The Battlefield, And Cell-Based Milk
startups-vc.summary: Recent notable startup transactions highlight emerging opportunities in underexplored sectors including a law firm operating system platform, defense technology for battlefield manufacturing, and cell-based milk production. These deals reflect investor interest in solving tangible problems in physical-world industries beyond traditional tech-focused verticals.

energy-climate.title: Solar and wind generated more electricity globally than gas power for the first time in April
energy-climate.summary: Wind and solar power generation reached a historic milestone in April 2026, producing 531 TWh of electricity globally and surpassing gas-fired power plants for the first time. The achievement, reported by energy think tank Ember, represents a significant turning point in the global energy transition away from fossil fuels.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 22 May 2026: UN adopts landmark resolution | Trump takes on 'RCP8.5' | Climate migration
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers major developments including a landmark UN resolution on climate action, political debate over climate scenarios, and discussions on climate-driven migration. The digest provides essential context on the week's most significant climate policy and scientific developments.

energy-climate.title: CCC: Investing in 'urgent' UK adaptation action 'cheaper than climate damages'
energy-climate.summary: The UK's Climate Change Committee reports that proactive investments in climate adaptation measures, including flood defences and cooling infrastructure, will cost substantially less than the damages incurred from failing to prepare for climate impacts. The analysis underscores the economic case for urgent adaptation spending.

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: Trump's false claims about the IPCC and 'RCP8.5' climate scenario
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief fact-checks recent claims by US President Trump regarding the IPCC and climate modeling scenarios, finding significant inaccuracies in his characterizations of climate science. The analysis clarifies the scientific basis and appropriate use of the RCP8.5 climate projection scenario.

energy-climate.title: Sungrow to supply 7.5GWh of storage for Masdar's Abu Dhabi RTC project
energy-climate.summary: Energy storage manufacturer Sungrow has secured a major contract to supply battery energy storage systems and PV inverters for Masdar's large-scale renewable energy project in Abu Dhabi. The agreement represents significant investment in grid-scale storage infrastructure in the Middle East.

energy-climate.title: AmpereHour and IndiGrid start up 180MW BESS in Gujarat
energy-climate.summary: AmpereHour Energy and IndiGrid have begun operations of a 180MW/360MWh battery energy storage facility in Gujarat, India, strengthening grid stability and enabling greater renewable energy integration. The project represents continued expansion of battery storage capacity in South Asia.

energy-climate.title: The Hydrogen Stream: Netherlands commissions 32-km hydrogen pipeline
energy-climate.summary: Gasunie has launched a 32-kilometer hydrogen pipeline in the Port of Rotterdam, marking the first operational section of the Dutch hydrogen backbone infrastructure. The project enables future expansion toward national and cross-border hydrogen networks, supporting Europe's hydrogen economy development.

energy-climate.title: EBRD lends €70 million to Slovenian BESS developer across four countries
energy-climate.summary: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development has provided a €70 million loan to Slovenian battery storage developer NGEN Energetske Rešitve to support merchant BESS projects across multiple Central European countries. The financing, backed by an EU first-loss guarantee, represents one of the first multi-country merchant battery storage portfolios in the region.

energy-climate.title: Guest post: How CMIP7 will shape the next wave of climate science
energy-climate.summary: Scientists are beginning work on CMIP7, the next generation of coordinated climate modeling projects involving hundreds of institutions worldwide. The initiative will establish the scientific foundation for future climate assessments and policy decisions.

energy-climate.title: Experts: Why migration is 'not a failure of adaptation' in a warming world
energy-climate.summary: Climate scientists convened in London to discuss how human migration serves as a legitimate adaptive response to climate change rather than a failure of adaptation efforts. The discussion reframes migration as a necessary climate resilience strategy in a warming world.

asia-pacific.title: 27 countries seek access to World Bank funds since Iran war
asia-pacific.summary:
Twenty-seven countries have moved since the Iran war started to put in place crisis instruments that could quickly access funding from existing World Bank programmes, according to an internal document viewed by Reuters.
The World Bank document did not name the ‌countries or the total amount of funds potentially being sought. The World Bank declined to comment.
The document showed that three countries had approved new instruments since the Middle East conflict began on February 28, while the...

asia-pacific.title: Japanese female prison’s rehabilitation goal faces health, language barriers
asia-pacific.summary:
Seated in a wheelchair, an elderly woman bends silently as her wrinkled fingers move with surprising speed to fold pieces of coloured origami paper into intricate shapes.
Beside her, another woman does the same, both adding their finished pieces to a pile on the table without looking up or exchanging a word.
Inside Tochigi Prison, where talking during work is forbidden, elderly women folding paper and sewing in silence reflect a broader test of Japan’s penal system: how to care for ageing,...

asia-pacific.title: How China wins by avoiding war
asia-pacific.summary:
Russian President Vladimir Putin arrived in Beijing in a much weaker position than he did more than four years ago, when he launched his invasion of Ukraine shortly after his last visit.
US President Donald Trump left Beijing after much fanfare. However, he was criticised back at home for failing to deliver on any key aspect of American interests, such as committing China to pressuring Iran to end the war on US terms. His popular support is at a historic low.
Putin, Trump and Chinese President..

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong’s first astronaut Lai Ka-ying picked for space station mission
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong will send its first astronaut to the Tiangong space station, the China Manned Space Agency announced on Saturday.
Lai Ka-ying, a police chief inspector and tech expert with a doctorate in computer science, will join the Shenzhou-23 mission as a payload specialist. She will become the fourth female astronaut to enter the Chinese space station.
The Shenzhou-23 crewed spacecraft is targeting a launch at 23:08 tomorrow.
According to the agency, she will work alongside astronauts Zhu...

asia-pacific.title: A cloud above the clouds: US, China race to make space a computing platform
asia-pacific.summary:
In 1957, the Soviet Union kicked off the Cold War’s space race, shocking the world by sending Sputnik, the world’s first artificial satellite, into orbit.
Four years later, it did the same to cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin, making him the first human in space. In 1969, the US provided another image that defined the era: Neil Armstrong leaving the Eagle lunar lander and planting the American flag on the moon, declaring it “one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.”
For the two superpowers, the.

asia-pacific.title: Ancient Chinese texts depict ‘immortal mirror’ akin to CT scan – an early notion of robots
asia-pacific.summary:
Ancient China dreamed of future technology long before the modern age, conjuring visions of magic mirrors, ingenious machines and even lifelike wooden puppets.
In the Tang dynasty (618–907) text Duyang Miscellaneous Records, a mysterious stone called the “immortal mirror” appears as a precursor to the CT scan.
Found in the southwest of an unnamed land, the polished stone was said to span hundreds of kilometres.

Passers-by could look into it like a mirror, but instead of reflecting their faces,.

asia-pacific.title: Graduates of top vocational school spared brunt of AI impact, new chairman says
asia-pacific.summary:
Graduates of Hong Kong’s largest vocational training institution have largely avoided the negative effects of AI and could reap more internship opportunities from a coming megaproject, the body’s new leader has said.
In an interview with the South China Morning Post, newly appointed Vocational Training Council (VTC) chairman Jeffrey Lam Kin-fung also vowed to review the courses to align them with emerging job opportunities.
The government earlier revealed that entry-level jobs for university...

asia-pacific.title: Southeast Asia is chasing the AI boom, but at what cost?
asia-pacific.summary:
In a swanky penthouse office outside Kuala Lumpur, tech firm Zetrix AI is putting a grand plan into motion to get 1 million people across Southeast Asia using its artificial intelligence agent, Avatar, by the end of the year.
Designed as an autonomous alter ego, Avatar is adept at dealing with tasks ranging from the mundane, like filling out forms, to the particular, such as helping influencers reply to thousands of social media messages.
The ultimate goal, according to the company’s Gen Z...

asia-pacific.title: SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on test flight
asia-pacific.summary:
SpaceX launched its biggest, most powerful Starship yet on a test flight Friday, an upgraded version that Nasa is counting on to land astronauts on the moon.
The redesigned mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he was taking the company public. It blasted off from the southern tip of Texas, carrying 20 mock Starlink satellites for release halfway around the world.
It is the 12th test flight of the rocket that Musk is building to get people to Mars one day. But.

asia-pacific.title: US judge tosses case against wrongly deported Salvadorean man Abrego Garcia
asia-pacific.summary:
A US federal judge dismissed a criminal case on Friday filed against a Salvadorean man at the centre of a row over US President Donald Trump’s crackdown on migrants.
US District Judge Waverly Crenshaw ruled that the indictment of Kilmar Abrego Garcia by the Trump administration’s Justice Department was “an abuse of prosecuting power”.
Abrego Garcia, a Maryland resident married to an American woman, was among more than 200 people sent to El Salvador’s CECOT mega-prison in March of last year.
The.
